name: Lindsay Wagner's Couscous with Tomato-Eggplant Sauce
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Fresh or canned tomatoes (no salt added); peeled and chopped
1 pound Whole-wheat couscous; cooked according to package directions
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
2. Add the onion and sauté until golden brown, stirring occasionally.
3. Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute more, stirring constantly.
4. Add the eggplant cubes and green pepper; stir and cook for 10 minutes or until the vegetables are tender.
5. Stir in the tomatoes, salt, pepper, paprika, basil, rosemary, oregano, parsley, and water. Mix well.
6. Bring to a simmer, cover, and cook for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking.
7. Using a fork, mash some of the eggplant or leave as chunks if desired.
8. Continue cooking, covered, for another 30 minutes.
9. Fluff the couscous with a fork and serve hot over the cooked couscous.
Per Serving:
Calories: 325 Protein: 11 grams Carbs: 66 grams
Sodium: 199 mg Fat: 2 grams (6% of calories)
Source: Cooking with the Stars
Published in Nutrition Action Healthletter
(Center for Science in the Public Interest)
